name: @vanillagreen/pi-caveman
description: Native Pi caveman communication mode with session persistence, slash command control, status badge, and settings-manager integration.

settings.label: Mode
settings.description: Default caveman mode. Off disables caveman; lite/full/ultra/micro enable the matching style. Session /caveman overrides win until session ends.

settings.label: Auto clarity escape
settings.description: Inject a full plain-prose system block when the user prompt names an explicit irreversible destructive operation (force-push, drop table, rm -rf, hard reset, etc.). Soft signals like 'confused' or 'security' no longer trigger this — the inline auto-clarity rule still lets the model self-elect plain prose for genuine destructive confirmations.

settings.label: Resume after clarity escape
settings.description: Keep caveman mode active after a temporary clarity escape.

settings.label: Allow session override
settings.description: Allow /caveman to override manager defaults in the current session.

settings.label: Normal code output
settings.description: Tell the model to keep code and code blocks normal.

settings.label: Normal commits
settings.description: Tell the model to keep commit messages normal unless explicitly asked otherwise.

settings.label: Normal reviews
settings.description: Tell the model to keep formal reviews/PR text normal unless explicitly asked otherwise.

settings.label: Normal external writes
settings.description: Tell the model to keep text destined for external systems normal: Linear/Jira/GitHub issue bodies and comments, PR/code-review comments, and chat messages (Slack/Discord/Teams/email). Caveman remains for in-chat replies only.

settings.label: Custom prompt suffix
settings.description: Extra instruction appended after the imperative caveman directives. Useful for project-specific guidance, but a contradicting suffix can weaken the directive — keep it consistent with the active mode.

author: vanillagreen
